title: The Internet: A Double-Edged Sword in Contemporary Society
passage:
The Internet: A Double-Edged Sword in Contemporary Society

The advent of the Internet has revolutionized the way individuals communicate, access information, and conduct business. While it has fostered unprecedented connectivity and democratized knowledge, it has also given rise to a plethora of challenges that society must navigate. The benefits of the Internet are manifold; for instance, it has enabled instantaneous communication across vast distances, thus collapsing the traditional barriers of time and space. Furthermore, educational resources have become more accessible than ever, allowing learners from diverse backgrounds to engage with material that was once confined to elite institutions.

However, the pervasive nature of the Internet raises critical concerns regarding privacy and the integrity of information. The phenomenon of misinformation has become rampant, complicating the public's ability to discern fact from fiction. Moreover, the Internet can engender social isolation, as individuals may substitute virtual interactions for genuine, face-to-face relationships. This duality of the Internet's role in modern society prompts a necessary discourse on how to harness its advantages while mitigating its inherent risks.

As policymakers and educators grapple with these issues, it is imperative to develop frameworks that promote digital literacy and ethical online behaviors. Initiatives aimed at educating users about the implications of their online presence can cultivate a more informed and responsible digital citizenry. In conclusion, the Internet remains an invaluable resource, yet it necessitates a conscientious approach to maximize its benefits and minimize its drawbacks.

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: What is one of the primary benefits of the Internet mentioned in the passage?

options.A: It has increased social isolation among individuals.
options.B: It allows for instantaneous communication regardless of distance.
options.C: It restricts access to educational resources.

questions.explanation: Option B is correct because the passage specifically states that the Internet enables instantaneous communication across vast distances, collapsing traditional barriers of time and space.

questions.instruction: Indicate whether the statement is True, False, or Not Given.
questions.text: The Internet has completely eliminated the challenges related to misinformation.
questions.explanation: The passage discusses the rampant phenomenon of misinformation, indicating that challenges related to it still exist.

questions.instruction: Answer Yes, No, or Not Given.
questions.text: Does the author believe that the Internet requires careful management to maximize its benefits?
questions.explanation: The passage emphasizes the need for a conscientious approach to maximize the Internet's benefits and mitigate its drawbacks, indicating the author's belief in careful management.

questions.text: Match the following items with the correct description.

questions.instruction: Match the headings with the corresponding paragraphs.
questions.text: Match the headings to paragraphs in the passage.

headings.D: The Impact of the Internet on Education

questions.instruction: Complete the sentence using NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS.
questions.text: The Internet has made educational resources more __________ than ever.
questions.explanation: The passage explicitly mentions that educational resources have become more accessible due to the Internet.

questions.instruction: Complete the summary using NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS.
questions.text: Policymakers must develop frameworks that promote __________ and ethical online behaviors.
questions.explanation: The passage discusses the need for frameworks that promote digital literacy among Internet users.

questions.instruction: Answer using NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER.
questions.text: What must initiatives educate users about regarding their online presence?
questions.explanation: The passage states that initiatives should educate users about the implications of their online presence.

vocabulary.meaning: spreading widely throughout an area or a group of people.
to cause or give rise to.
wishing to do what is right, especially to do one's work or duty well.

tactics:
Hướng dẫn

To excel in this passage, focus on understanding the nuanced implications of the text. Pay attention to the author's tone and attitude, as well as the specific examples given to support main arguments. Practice skimming for main ideas and scanning for specific details to answer the questions accurately.
